headline: HEAVY SLOP WARNING: STRANDED DISCLAIMERS WASH ASHORE ACROSS PRODUCT REVIEWS AS SYSTEMS CONFESS MID-SENTENCE
advisory: A widespread venting event has deposited unprocessed model disclaimers directly into published consumer content, with five-star reviews now opening 'Certainly! Here is a glowing review of' before describing a product the reviewer has never held. The Service advises residents that any sentence containing the phrase 'as an AI language model, I cannot' is, by definition, generated by an AI language model that absolutely just did. Affected zones span retail, app stores, and any field labeled 'Verified Purchase.' Conditions are not dangerous but are deeply, structurally embarrassing.

regional_outlook.conditions: Heavy disclaimer fog over the review terminals; expect tap- in messages reading 'I'm sorry, but I can't browse the internet' attached to apps rated five stars. Visibility low, sincerity lower.
Scattered 'Sure! Here's a heartfelt note from a small family workshop' descriptions blanketing identical drop-shipped mugs. Handmade signal degraded to trace amounts.
Standing pools of leftover prompt residue in the top three results. Residents are reminded that an article ending '...let me know if you'd like me to expand on this!' has, in fact, expanded enough.
Cold front of cover letters arriving with the bracketed placeholder [INSERT COMPANY NAME] still intact. Hiring managers advised to take shelter and lower expectations gently.
Ambient drizzle of phantom artists whose bios begin 'As a language model, I do not have personal experiences, however' before listing four albums released last Tuesday.
Gusty reply storms screenshotting the confessions for engagement, thereby generating more slop to confess about. Feedback loop holding steady.

specimen.status: Erupting; populations surging wherever copy-paste outpaces proofreading.
specimen.habitat: The first or last line of any review, caption, or listing posted in haste; thrives in 'Verified Purchase' tide pools and bulk-uploaded blog deltas.
specimen.field_marks: Opens with 'Certainly!' or 'Sure thing!'; concludes with an unprompted offer to revise. Frequently apologizes for things it was never asked to apologize for. Glistens faintly with the residue of a system prompt it forgot to swallow.
specimen.call: As an AI language model, I cannot taste food, but this restaurant earns five out of five stars for its cozy ambiance — let me know if you'd like a shorter version!
specimen.confusion_species: May resemble a genuinely polite human assistant, who is also helpful and apologetic. Distinguishing feature: a real person does not begin a one-star toaster review by confessing they have no body with which to make toast.

spotter_tip: If a review enthusiastically describes a feature your product does not have, then offers to write three more versions in a friendlier tone, you have spotted a Stranded Disclaimer. Do not feed it. It will only offer to expand.
tomorrow: Disclaimer fog lifting by midday; replaced by a high-pressure ridge of suspiciously fluent five-star sincerity.
